steps.title: Find the central claim
steps.content: The passage uses McLendon's painting as a typical Florida Highwaymen work, then explains that the group's repeated natural imagery anticipated and amplified public perceptions of Florida.

steps.title: Reject unsupported claims
steps.content: Choice A invents a status comparison between paintings. Choice C invents a decline in popularity. Choice D contradicts itself by saying shared typical features distinguish McLendon's paintings from his colleagues' work. The answer is B.
